title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh from Braintree is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh underwent chemo, surgeries, and physical therapy before returning to running.
Inspired by friends and mentors, she continues to run to honor loved ones and prove cancer doesn't define her.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and others warn of privacy risks with Meta's facial recognition in smart glasses.
Lawmakers urge Meta to clarify plans for biometric data collection and user consent by April 6.
A series of protests, including 'No Kings' rallies, are scheduled in Massachusetts on March 28.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council postponed a vote on a $2.65 million plan to replace firefighting gear containing PFAS.
PFAS, linked to cancer, were found in current firefighting gear, prompting calls for safer equipment.
Firefighters and union criticized the delay, citing health risks; councilors cited budget concerns and durability questions.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro proposes turning Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve due to $57 million in needed repairs and rising costs.
The city aims to cut long-term expenses by focusing on native wildlife and conservation, with limited free access starting in the fall.
Zoo officials express concerns over the transition, emphasizing stability for animals and staff during the proposal's consideration.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown arranged a spontaneous microwedding in Somerville's Bow Market due to a blizzard.
FX's 'Love Story' series about JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette prompts nostalgia for 1990s communication.
Contemporary use of 'partner' in relationships is discussed, exploring its meaning and implications.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, from Burlington, MA, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH.
Averback's father was treated for glioblastoma at MGH and passed away in 2020.
After surviving testicular cancer, Averback trains to honor his father and his own recovery.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots show interest in trading for Eagles WR A.J. Brown, offer declined amid June 1 cap space strategy.
A.J. Brown works out with new Patriots safety Kevin Byard, fueling trade speculation due to their past Titans connection.
Eagles seek first-round pick or more for Brown, following high-value Jaylen Waddle trade between Broncos and Dolphins.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins prospect James Hagens tallied 47 points in 34 games for Boston College in his sophomore season.
As the BC season ends, anticipation grows for James Hagens to sign an entry-level contract with the Boston Bruins.
At 5-foot-11 and 193 pounds, Hagens is expected to bring playmaking ability and speed to the Bruins' lineup.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' starring Will Arnett, directed by Bradley Cooper, is now streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' featuring Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan is available on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl,' a docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, is streaming on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora reflects on his future as Red Sox manager, emphasizing family priorities and contract expiration in 2027.
Cora needs 134 more wins to surpass Terry Francona's Red Sox managerial win record of 744.
Cora has experienced career highs and lows, including a World Series win and a suspension following the Astros scandal.
